title: Bitter Flaky Uruguayan Cod Broth
description: A savory, bitter broth with flaky cod, enriched by tarragon leaves and a gentle saute, perfect as a soothing hangover cure.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large stockpot over medium heat.
Add diced onion, minced garlic, sliced carrot, and celery; sauté until vegetables soften, about 5 minutes.
Pour in 2 liters of cold water and add bay leaf, tarragon leaves, kosher salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook for 25 minutes to develop flavor.
Add the cod fillets to the broth, cover, and simmer gently for 10 minutes or until the fish flakes easily (internal temp 145°F/63°C).
Remove the pot from heat and let it sit, covered, for 5 minutes to allow the fish to finish cooking through steam.
Using a slotted spoon, carefully lift the cod fillets out and set aside, keeping the broth in the pot.
Reduce the broth over medium heat for another 5 minutes to slightly thicken.
Stir in lemon juice, adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
Serve the broth hot, garnished with fresh parsley, and enjoy the refreshing, bitter flavor intended to soothe hangovers.
